items.change_action: must equal mapped constant for Heartbeat demultiplexing
items.comment: BoringSSL ?? Heartbeat content type/???????? Heartbeat ????/????????????? RFC ? 5 ? Heartbeat demux ?????????
items.comparison_summary: ???? OCT 24 ? DTLS <1.3 Heartbeat????? SSL3_RT_HEARTBEAT ??? Heartbeat ????type 24 ?? DTLSPlaintext ????????????????????? handshake/ACK/application_data/alert ???????? unexpected record ?????????????????? Heartbeat ??????????? Heartbeat ????????
items.standard_check: RFC 9147 Figure 5 maps OCT 24 and decrypted content type 24 to Heartbeat, but Heartbeat is a separate optional TLS/DTLS feature rather than a mandatory DTLS 1.3 core handshake path.
items.code_check: Static re-read found no SSL3_RT_HEARTBEAT or heartbeat handler. DTLS record parsing accepts the outer shape, but d1_both.cc and d1_pkt.cc only dispatch handshake, ACK, application data, alert, and CCS-specific paths.
items.test_check: focused_static_id152_153_185_187.py PASS: no heartbeat symbols were found and non-handshake dispatch paths do not route type 24 to a handler.
items.decision_reason: Confirmed partial: unsupported Heartbeat is safely rejected/not dispatched, but the implementation cannot process Heartbeat records if that feature were required by the deployment.

items.change_action: must equal mapped constant for encrypted_record with CID demultiplexing
items.comment: BoringSSL ?? DTLS 1.2 CID/tls12_cid(25) ?????? CID demux?DTLS 1.3 ???????? C bit?
items.comparison_summary: ?? OCT 25 ??? DTLSCiphertext with CID (DTLS 1.2)????? tls12_cid ? connection_id ?????parse_dtls13_record ? C bit ??????? false??????? C=0??????? CID demux/?????
items.standard_check: RFC 9147 Figure 5 reserves OCT 25 for DTLSCiphertext with CID (DTLS 1.2), and Section 4.1 describes mixed DTLS 1.2/1.3 demultiplexing when CIDs are in use.
items.code_check: Static re-read found no tls12_cid/connection_id implementation. parse_dtls13_record returns false when the C bit is set, and the sender fixes C=0.
items.test_check: focused_static_id152_153_185_187.py PASS: confirmed absence of CID symbols and presence of explicit C-bit rejection/fixed C=0 send header.
items.decision_reason: Confirmed unsatisfied for CID-capable operation: the record layer has no DTLS 1.2 CID demux path and rejects DTLS 1.3 CID headers.

items.change_condition: in some cases it may be necessary to send an ACK which does not contain any record numbers
items.evidence_from_input: it may be necessary to send an ACK which does not contain any record numbers.
items.comment: BoringSSL ?? ACK ????? vector???????? records_to_ack ?????????? RFC ??????? ACK ?????
items.comparison_summary: ???????????? record number ? ACK?CBB ?????? vector?? dtls1_schedule_ack ? sending_ack ??? !records_to_ack.empty()?send_ack ?????? ACK ?? MTU?????? ACK ?????????????
items.standard_check: RFC 9147 Section 7.1 says some cases may require an ACK with no record numbers, such as receiving EncryptedExtensions before ServerHello.
items.code_check: ACK encoding could represent an empty vector, but dtls1_schedule_ack only sets sending_ack when records_to_ack is non-empty. send_ack also assumes room for at least one 16-byte RecordNumber.
items.test_check: focused_static_id157_empty_ack.py PASS: confirmed sending_ack is gated by !records_to_ack.empty() and no empty-ACK scheduling path exists.
items.decision_reason: Confirmed partial: regular ACK generation works, but the special empty ACK shortcut is not implemented.

items.evidence_from_input: If it is set to "cid_spare", then either an existing or new CID MAY be used.
items.comparison_summary: ?? NewConnectionId.usage=cid_spare ??????? CID ???? CID????? RequestConnectionId/NewConnectionId/ConnectionIdUsage ???DTLS 1.3 ???????? CID ??? C bit????????
items.standard_check: RFC 9147 Section 9 defines NewConnectionId.usage=cid_spare and says either an existing or new CID may be used.
items.code_check: No NewConnectionId or ConnectionIdUsage parser/state exists. The DTLS 1.3 sender never sends CID and the receiver rejects C bit.
items.test_check: focused_static_id152_153_185_187.py PASS: confirmed absence of NewConnectionId/RequestConnectionId/cid_spare symbols and CID-capable record support.
items.decision_reason: Confirmed unsatisfied: BoringSSL cannot process cid_spare because DTLS CID update support is absent.

items.evidence_from_input: If usage is set to "cid_immediate", then one of the new CIDs MUST be used immediately for all future records.
items.comparison_summary: ?? usage=cid_immediate ???????????? CID??????? C=0 no Connection ID??? NewConnectionId ???? CID ????????????
items.standard_check: RFC 9147 Section 9 says usage=cid_immediate means one of the new CIDs MUST be used immediately for all future records.
items.code_check: No CID selection or immediate-switch state exists. The sender writes C=0 and no Connection ID in every DTLS 1.3 encrypted record.
items.test_check: focused_static_id152_153_185_187.py PASS: confirmed fixed C=0 header and no cid_immediate symbol/path.
items.decision_reason: Confirmed unsatisfied: there is no mechanism to switch future records to a new CID immediately.

items.related_state_or_step: Sending NewConnectionId in response to RequestConnectionId
items.evidence_from_input: Endpoints SHOULD respond to RequestConnectionId by sending a NewConnectionId with usage "cid_spare" containing num_cids CIDs as soon as possible.
items.comparison_summary: ??? RequestConnectionId ???? usage=cid_spare ? NewConnectionId?????? request_connection_id/new_connection_id handshake type ????????????? CID????????
items.standard_check: RFC 9147 Section 9 says endpoints SHOULD respond to RequestConnectionId by sending NewConnectionId with usage cid_spare containing num_cids CIDs as soon as possible.
items.code_check: No RequestConnectionId handshake type handling or NewConnectionId response generation exists in the DTLS handshake code.
items.test_check: focused_static_id152_153_185_187.py PASS: confirmed absent request_connection_id/new_connection_id handling symbols.
items.decision_reason: Confirmed unsatisfied: RequestConnectionId cannot trigger a cid_spare NewConnectionId response.
